steps.title: Identify what the narrator finds amusing
steps.content: The narrator watches people work out through a gym window and says, there ain't nothing funnier than that, then treats the sight as entertainment—humorously naming it The About to Pass Out Show.

steps.title: Evaluate choices against the signal
steps.content: Choice B describes the narrator's amusement about watching people work out—exactly what the passage focuses on. The others misplace the context (A), invent a nonexistent problem (C), or confuse a humorous title for a real theater experience (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
